stories.headline: South Korea's AI Stocks Crash, Halting Trading on Kospi
stories.summary: Heavyweights Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, which dominate the Kospi index, fell approximately 9% each, dragging the broader market down by 8%. The sharp decline in AI-related equities forced the exchange to trigger circuit breakers, halting trading for roughly 20 minutes.
stories.original_title: South Korea’s favourite AI stocks slide again, drag Kospi index down 8% — Heavyweights Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, which together account for more than half of Kospi's market cap, fell around 9% each on Friday, forcing the exchange to trigger circuit breakers and halt trading for roughly 20 minutes.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows how risky the current AI boom is. When big tech companies like Samsung drop in value, it can wipe out billions of dollars from investors' savings very quickly. It warns us that betting everything on AI is a high-stakes gamble that can crash just as fast as it rises.
stories.other_side: Some investors see this crash as a good chance to buy stocks at a discount. They believe AI is the future and that prices will bounce back up soon, making this a temporary setback rather than a permanent loss.

stories.headline: OpenAI IPO Delay Sends SoftBank and Tech Stocks Tumbling
stories.summary: Reports that OpenAI is leaning toward delaying its initial public offering until next year have caused significant volatility in the tech sector. SoftBank shares plummeted as a major investor, while other tech giants like Oracle also saw their stock prices drop amid growing fears about the timeline for OpenAI's market entry.
stories.original_title: SoftBank’s Shares Tumble After Report of OpenAI’s IPO Delay - Bloomberg.com — Bloomberg.com OpenAI Leans Toward Waiting Until Next Year for I.P.O.The New York Times OpenAI IPO Fears Drag Down Oracle and Other Tech StocksBarron's OpenAI leans toward waiting until next year for I…
stories.why_it_matters: OpenAI is one of the most important players in artificial intelligence. Delaying its public listing means there is less new money flowing into the tech market right now, which makes other tech stocks feel nervous and less valuable. It slows down the excitement around AI investments for now.
stories.other_side: Critics argue that delaying the launch allows OpenAI to fix any remaining problems before going public. They believe a stronger, more stable company will be worth more money in the long run than rushing to sell shares too early.

stories.headline: Micron Earnings Reveal the High Price Tag of the AI Boom
stories.summary: Micron's exceptional earnings report highlighted the escalating costs associated with the artificial intelligence boom, showing how memory chipmakers are capitalizing on severe supply bottlenecks. The data underscores the financial burden AI demand places on big tech companies and device buyers as infrastructure costs skyrocket.
stories.original_title:
The AI boom now has a price tag — and Micron just sent the bill: Chart of the Day — Micron's blowout earnings did not just revive the AI trade. They showed how expensive it is becoming.
The memory chipmaker is getting paid first because AI demand is running into a supply bottleneck. The harder question is who pays next: Big Tech, device buye…
stories.why_it_matters: Making AI requires expensive computer chips, and Micron’s report proves these costs are going up fast. This means companies building AI tools have to spend more money, which could lead to higher prices for the apps and services we use every day. The cost of the AI revolution is becoming harder to ignore.
stories.other_side: Supporters say that high demand proves AI is useful and necessary. They argue that spending more on better chips leads to faster, smarter technology that saves time and money in other areas, making the extra cost worth it.

stories.headline: FinOps Strategies Evolve to Manage Soaring AI Token Costs
stories.summary: Chief Information Officers are increasingly turning to FinOps frameworks to navigate the complex and rapidly scaling costs of AI adoption. As usage-based tokenomics become prevalent, organizations must balance aggressive AI integration with strict spending controls to prevent budget overruns.
stories.original_title: FinOps for AI: How CIOs are navigating tokenomics — CIOs must balance AI adoption with controlled spending -- a feat complicated by fast-scaling, usage-based AI costs. FinOps is expanding its scope to help manage that complexity.
stories.why_it_matters: Using AI costs a lot of money, especially when you pay for every small action or "token." Companies are now having to watch their budgets closely to avoid running out of cash. This forces businesses to be smarter about which AI tools they use and how much they spend.
stories.other_side: Some experts believe that strict budget controls might slow down innovation. If companies cut back on AI spending to save money, they might fall behind competitors who are willing to spend freely to build better features.

stories.headline: Robotics Startup FieldAI Surpasses $100 Million Revenue Milestone
stories.summary: FieldAI, a developer of software enabling robots to operate in hazardous environments like mines and construction sites, has achieved $100 million in revenue. Backed by investors including Bezos Expeditions, the company's growth signals increasing commercial viability for industrial robotics solutions.
stories.original_title:
Robotics startup FieldAI has hit a $100 million milestone — FieldAI builds software for all kinds of robots.
FieldAI
FieldAI says it has topped $100 million in revenue and customer contracts.
The startup's software helps robots operate in mines, on construction sites, and in factories.
Investors, including Bezos Exped…
stories.why_it_matters: FieldAI’s success shows that robots are no longer just science fiction; they are making real money in dangerous jobs. This proves that investing in industrial robots is a safe bet for the future of work. It encourages more companies to use robots to keep humans safe from harm.
stories.other_side: Workers worry that these robots will take their jobs away, especially in tough industries like mining. They fear that automation will leave many people unemployed without enough new opportunities to replace those lost roles.

stories.headline: Wall Street Chip Stocks Slide Despite Previous Rally
stories.summary: U.S. chip stocks retreated sharply as futures tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq led Wall Street losses, reversing a recent rally driven by Micron. Apple's price hikes have stoked fears that high costs may dampen the AI trade, putting renewed pressure on semiconductor manufacturers.
stories.original_title:
Wall St futures fall as chip stocks resume slide after Micron-led rally — U.S. chip stocks retreat after Apple price hikes stoke fears over AI trade
June 26 (Reuters) - Futures tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq led Wall Street losses on Friday, as chip stocks came under renewed pressure after a sharp rally in the previous session, whil…
stories.why_it_matters: Chip makers rely on selling to big phone and computer brands. When Apple raises prices, people buy fewer devices, which means chip companies sell fewer parts. This creates a ripple effect that hurts the entire tech supply chain and lowers stock values.
stories.other_side: Investors think this dip is just a short pause in a long upward trend. They believe that demand for AI and smart devices will grow again soon, so buying now is a smart move before prices go back up.

stories.headline: Toyota-Backed Air Taxi Achieves Critical 100 km/h Flight Milestone
stories.summary: SkyDrive's SD-05 multicopter, backed by Toyota, successfully demonstrated stable flight at 100 km/h, proving its wingless design can handle real aerodynamic loads. This achievement advances Japan's JCAB certification process and keeps the company on track for a potential commercial launch in 2028.
stories.original_title: Toyota-backed air taxi hits 100 km/h, speeding toward commercial debut — Toyota-backed SkyDrive's SD-05 multicopter has achieved stable flight at 100 km/h (62 mph), proving its wingless design handles real aerodynamic loads. The milestone feeds Japan's JCAB certification process and keeps 2028 commercial launch in sight, if regula…
stories.why_it_matters: Flying taxis could change how we travel by cutting down traffic jams in cities. Reaching higher speeds safely is a major step toward making this real within the next few years. It opens the door for a new kind of transportation that is faster and possibly cleaner.
stories.other_side: Many people are worried about the noise and safety risks of flying vehicles over neighborhoods. There are also concerns about who can afford these rides, fearing they will only be for the rich while regular people stay stuck in traffic.

stories.headline: Google Gemini's New Computer Control Feature Sparks Security Concerns
stories.summary: Google has enabled Gemini to control user computers directly, but security experts warn that this capability exposes AI agents to hidden traps on the open web. Hackers are already targeting these agentic systems, raising urgent questions about the safety of autonomous AI navigation.
stories.original_title:
Google Gemini Can Now Control Your Computer. Hackers Are Already Targeting AI Agents via @sejournal, @martinibuster — Google warns that websites can expose AI agents to hidden traps for agentic AI navigating the open web.
The post Google Gemini Can Now Control Your Computer. Hackers Are Already Targeting AI Agents appeared first in Search Engine Journal.
stories.why_it_matters: Giving AI control over your computer is convenient but very dangerous. Hackers can trick the AI into doing harmful things, like stealing passwords or deleting files. This highlights a major security flaw that needs to be fixed before we trust AI with our personal data.
stories.other_side: Tech companies argue that users can control these settings and choose not to give AI full access. They believe that with proper warnings and user choices, the benefits of having an AI assistant outweigh the potential risks.

stories.headline: Global Firms Commit $90 Billion to India Amid Geopolitical Shifts
stories.summary: Major global corporations have pledged over $90 billion in investments in India, with Amazon leading with $48 billion committed through 2030. These massive inflows, including significant contributions from AirTrunk and Saint-Gobain, reflect a strategic pivot toward India despite broader geopolitical uncertainties.
stories.original_title: Global firms commit over $90 bn investment in India amid geopolitical uncertainty — Among the biggest investments are $48 billion by Amazon up to 2030, $30 billion by Australian data centre operator AirTrunk and 1 billion euros by Saint-Gobain
stories.why_it_matters: India is becoming a major hub for global business, attracting huge amounts of foreign money. This investment creates jobs and helps build better infrastructure in the country. It also shows that companies are looking for new places to grow as markets in other countries become crowded.
stories.other_side: Local critics worry that foreign companies will take over local businesses and push out small Indian entrepreneurs. They fear that the profits will mostly go back to foreign headquarters instead of staying in the local economy.

stories.headline: AIM Discontinued: Secure Alternatives for Modern Messaging
stories.summary: With AOL Instant Messenger officially discontinued, users are migrating to secure alternatives that prioritize privacy and encryption. This shift highlights the ongoing evolution of communication technologies as legacy platforms fade and new security-focused messaging services gain traction.
stories.original_title: AIM Discontinued – 6 Secure Alternatives to AIM — AIM, aka AOL Instant Messenger, is now a relic of the past, but if you’re searching for the best alternatives to AIM, you can keep the chat going with these 6 alternative messaging services. While each of our featured messengers is geared for security to keep…
stories.why_it_matters: Old messaging apps are disappearing, forcing everyone to move to newer, more secure platforms. This shift protects our private conversations from being read by hackers or advertisers. It marks the end of an era and the start of a focus on digital privacy.
stories.other_side: Some users miss the simplicity and familiarity of the old apps. They find the new secure options confusing to use or worry that the new platforms might collect their data in different ways, even if they claim to be private.
